rationale: In the citing paper, this reference is used to support a concrete theoretical mechanism rather than as a generic background mention. Specifically, Bowman and Narayandas (2004) is cited in the H5 argument to justify that major customers place heavy time and resource demands on suppliers, which can disperse marketing activities across units. That claim is then used to explain how customer power may constrain CMO discretion and influence firm-value effects. Even though the citation appears briefly, it meaningfully informs hypothesis development.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Bowman and Narayandas (2004) as supporting evidence that major customers can absorb organizational marketing resources and attention. It transfers that insight into its own CMO-discretion framework to explain when customer power weakens the value impact of CMO appointments.
